introSection: Tiger Terror was an indoor steel kiddie roller coaster located at Safari Land in Villa Park, Illinois, USA.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The coaster opened with the rest of the family entertainment center on September 17, 2002.

In October 2024, it was reported that the coaster would close eventually to be replaced. The coaster was originally set to close in February 2025, but the closure was postponed due to the ride's replacement, Congo Cruizer, still being tested at its manufacturer's headquarters.

The coaster's final day of operation was confirmed on August 15, 2025 to be August 17 of that same year.

sections.title: Ride experience
sections.content: The train climbs the 22-foot spiral lift hill and picks up speed as it travels down a shallow drop. Following the drop, the train enters a left turnaround, which leads to a bunny hill. The train then takes two additional left-hand turnarounds and returns to the station after three laps of the layout is complete.

fullText:
Tiger Terror was an indoor steel kiddie roller coaster located at Safari Land in Villa Park, Illinois, USA.

The coaster opened with the rest of the family entertainment center on September 17, 2002.

In October 2024, it was reported that the coaster would close eventually to be replaced. The coaster was originally set to close in February 2025, but the closure was postponed due to the ride's replacement, Congo Cruizer, still being tested at its manufacturer's headquarters.

The coaster's final day of operation was confirmed on August 15, 2025 to be August 17 of that same year.



The train climbs the 22-foot spiral lift hill and picks up speed as it travels down a shallow drop. Following the drop, the train enters a left turnaround, which leads to a bunny hill. The train then takes two additional left-hand turnarounds and returns to the station after three laps of the layout is complete.
